title: Best TVs for PS5 and PS5 Pro in 2025
author: Elliot Stent
excerpt: The PS5 is an amazing console and a gateway to some of the world's greatest games. It packs all kinds of next-level features to elevate the way you play...
content:
The PS5 is an amazing console and a gateway to some of the world's greatest games. It packs all kinds of next-level features to elevate the way you play, like 4K support at 120Hz and VRR (Variable Refresh Rate) for sharp, stutter-free graphics. Plus ALLM (Auto Low Latency Mode), which reduces input lag so every button press feels instant.

But to really unlock everything your PlayStation 5 can do — you'll need a TV that can keep up. And if your current telly isn't cutting it, this best TVs for PS5 guide is your shortcut to finding the perfect match for your system. Whether you're on a budget or after an 8K powerhouse for your PS5 Pro.

## Guide map:

![a white playstation 5 console sitting on a table in a living room in front of a tv](/images/articles/best-tvs-for-ps5/image-001.webp)

## Key features to look for in a PS5 TV

Choosing the best gaming TV for PS5 isn't just about going for the biggest screen — it's all about getting the most out of your console. With that in mind, here are some of the key features to look out for:

- **4K Resolution:** The PS5 outputs at a 4K resolution. That's about 8 million pixels, and four times more than Full HD! So, 4K TVs that also display at this resolution will make sure you're getting the sharpest visuals possible.

- **High Refresh Rate (120Hz):** The higher the refresh rate, the smoother your console games will look. The PS5 outputs at up to a whopping 120fps (frames per second), so choose a TV that supports 120Hz to equal this. It'll make a big difference in fast-paced F1 games.

- **HDMI 2.1 Support:** This is the latest HDMI standard and allows for faster data transfer rates. HDMI 2.1 is important for PS5 features like 4K at 120fps and Variable Refresh Rate (VRR), which syncs the TV's refresh rate with the game's frame rate to reduce screen tearing.

- **HDR support:** TVs with High Dynamic Range capture more detail in both the bright and dark areas of the screen, making scenes look more realistic. A PS5 comes out of the box HDR-ready, so pairing it with an HDR TV will really bring your fave PS5 games to life.

- **Low Input Lag:** Input lag is the delay between pressing a button on your controller and seeing the action on the screen. The lower this is, the more responsive everything will feel. We'd suggest going for a TV with an input lag of 20ms or less for the best performance.

## Overall best TV for PS5

There are loads of picture-perfect TVs out there that'll take your PS5 gameplay up a notch. But the LG C5 65" OLED evo AI 4K HDR Smart TV is a winning screen for many reasons.

With its big display, vast virtual landscapes in games like _Death Stranding 2: On The Beach_ and _Borderlands 4_ will feel seriously immersive. And its OLED screen tech features self-lit pixels, so you'll get perfect blacks and razor-sharp detail. That'll make it easier to spot enemies lurking in the shadows.

This telly's got all the speed you'll need too. With 144Hz VRR and ALLM baked in, plus a rapid 0.1ms response time, you can play without any lag or motion blur spoiling the fun. LG's built-in AI also works behind the scenes to tweak your picture and sound in real-time. So, whether you're deep in a campaign or battling online, your games will always look and sound their best.

![a large lg c5 tv with a picture of a fox on a wall in a living room](/images/articles/best-tvs-for-ps5/image-002.webp)

## Best PS5 TV for bright rooms

Sunrays and ceiling lights bouncing off your TV's screen can make it tough to see what's going on. And let's face it, no one wants to have to squint through their favourite game! But you can see everything clearly on a TV like the Samsung QN90D 85" Neo QLED. And no, it's got nothing to do with how big it is...

The QN90D's Quantum Mini LEDs light up the display with vivid colours that can outshine midday sun or glowing lamps. While in the background, the NQ4 AI Gen2 Processor adjusts brightness levels to keep your picture clear. But it's the anti-reflection screen that really makes this TV epic in well-lit rooms. It absorbs light to reduce distracting reflections, helping you stay in the zone.

Love a bit of friendly competition? With its wide viewing angle, you can invite your mates over for a few matches on EA Sports FC games and they'll get a clear view no matter where they're sitting. The QN90D's lightning-quick Motion Xcelerator kicks in to keep motion blur and input lag at bay too. So, you'll never lose sight of the ball.

![a samsung qn90d tv with a green screen on a green background](/images/articles/best-tvs-for-ps5/image-003.webp)

## Best premium TV for PS5

If you think a top console like the PS5 deserves a top TV, then the LG G5 65" OLED evo AI 4K HDR Smart TV ticks all of the boxes.

It has LG's Brightness Booster Ultimate built right in, helping this OLED panel punch through glare. That means things will look just as awesome at 2pm as they do during a midnight gaming marathon. And with Dolby Vision onboard, every shadow, explosion, and cinematic cutscene is tuned to perfection.

Gaming performance is where the G5 really flexes though. You get 4K at 144Hz, ultra-low input lag, plus support for VRR. These features make this telly a smooth operator for intense action games and competitive play. And LG's Game Optimiser puts all your settings in one place — just load it up, tweak away, then get back to the action at the touch of a button.

![a gaming room with a computer desk and a chair and a big lg g5 tv on the wall](/images/articles/best-tvs-for-ps5/image-004.webp)

## Best Sony TV for PS5

The Sony Bravia 8 II 65" QD-OLED 4K HDR Smart TV is designed with the PlayStation 5 in mind. As soon as you plug in your console, its Game Mode will kick in automatically to cut down lag. The Auto HDR Tone Mapping feature adjusts your picture settings with no menu-diving needed too.

But if you do want to change things up, just launch Game Menu 2. Here, you can quickly access features like Motion Blur Reduction and a Black Equalizer that brightens your screen (perfect for horror games). There's even a Screen Size feature that can scale things down into a smaller window for a tighter field of view. That's handy for first-person shooters where sharp reflexes rule.

Add in 4K at 120Hz and support for VRR, and you've got a setup that's ready for next-gen adventures right out of the box.

![a sony bravia 8 ii oled tv showing characters from the astro bot game on its screen](/images/articles/best-tvs-for-ps5/image-005.webp)

## Best TV for cinematic PS5 games

If you want your PS5 epics to look and feel like Hollywood blockbusters, the Panasonic Z95B 77" OLED is an awesome screen for the job. Its huge display is super immersive, and with OLED tech, it keeps an incredible level of contrast across every inch. That means it'll be light and dark in all the right places to up the realism. Plus, you'll never miss hidden paths in titles like _God of War Ragnarok_ or _Final Fantasy VII Rebirth_.

The 4-layer panel design boosts overall brightness too, so even the murkiest corners of Midgard will pop with detail. And with Game Mode Extreme and the HCX Pro AI Mk II Processor working together like a perfect co-op pairing, you'll get ultra-low latency and tear-free graphics.

The sound of this TV pulls you in too. That's because the Z95B's 360 Soundscape Pro system uses up-firing and side-firing speakers with Dolby Atmos to wrap you in the action. Every axe swing, spell cast, and orchestral swell should hit with cinematic precision.

![a person holding a video game controller in front of a video game](/images/articles/best-tvs-for-ps5/image-006.webp)

## Best mid-range TV for PS5

The clue's in the name with the LG B5 55" OLED AI 4K HDR Smart TV. It's got pretty much all the key features we listed that your PS5 needs, like 4K and HDR for out-of-this-world clarity, contrast, and colours. But best of all, this TV won't break the bank.

Its self-lit OLED panel delivers perfect contrast and shades, so every shadowy corridor and sun-drenched battlefield looks lifelike. Plus, with support for 4K at 120Hz and an ultra-fast 0.1ms response time, it'll all look and feel fluid — whether you're dodging enemies or drifting through _Gran Turismo 7_. Even older games can get a glow-up with AI Super Upscaling.

And if you fancy a break from your PS5 adventures, you can open up the Gaming Portal. It lets you play thousands of extra games directly on the telly, with access to GeForce NOW, Amazon Luna, Blacknut, and now even the Xbox app. Fire up AAA titles, or chill with casual games you can play with just the remote.

![a light living room with a large lg b5 tv on the wall](/images/articles/best-tvs-for-ps5/image-007.webp)

## Best budget TV for PS5

You don't have to spend loads on a gaming TV for your PS5. There are plenty of affordable options out there that tick most of the boxes, like the TCL C6K 55" QD-Mini LED 4K HDR Smart TV. It's not as big as some of the other TVs on our list, but with a refresh rate that can ramp up to 144Hz — you'll be able to jump, drift, and shoot without any blur slowing you down.

The moment you power on your console, Game Master activates to slash latency and dial in the perfect settings. ALLM and VRR support means you'll also get ultra-responsive gameplay and visuals that stay crisp even when the action heats up.

But it's not just about speed — it's about spectacle too. The QLED panel can show over a billion colours, so every explosion, shadow, and cutscene will look super vivid. And if you need a break between long gaming sessions? Just put your controller down and load up the Google TV app to kick back with your fave shows.

![a painting of a person riding on a rocket coming out of a tv](/images/articles/best-tvs-for-ps5/image-008.webp)

## Best small TV for PS5

Short on space but big on gaming? The Panasonic Z90B 42" OLED 4K HDR Smart Fire TV is perfect for a cosy gaming den or office. It's stacked with features that can make quests and competitive matches feel more immersive on its smaller screen.

With Game Mode Extreme, the TV lowers input lag and ramps up the refresh rate to keep graphics smooth. While Game Sound Mode boosts audio cues to match the genre of game you're playing. Fire up the FPS setting, and you'll hear every footstep or incoming attack with pinpoint precision. Perfect for _Call of Duty_ games!

Want to keep tabs on performance? Just open the Game Control Board. It'll show you key stats like frame rate and input lag. And you can even you turn on other settings like a Dark Visibility Enhancer to see things more clearly in haunting horror titles like _Silent Hill F_.

![a person playing a video game on a flat screen panasonic z90b tv](/images/articles/best-tvs-for-ps5/image-009.webp)

## Best 8K TV for PS5 Pro

Launched in late 2024, the PS5 Pro is Sony's most advanced gaming console ever. It's got a bigger GPU and Ai-driven upscaling, which enhances PlayStation games with more realistic lighting effects and smoother frame rates. But it supports 8K gaming too, which means you can plug it into an 8K TV and experience the most incredible graphics. We're talking about 33 million pixels!

If you've got a Pro, the Samsung QN900F 65" Neo QLED 8K Mini-LED TV can really show off what it can do. Powered by Samsung's NQ8 AI Gen2 processor, it upscales every pixel in real time. So, even non-8K games will look sharper and more detailed.

Plus, with support for refresh rates up to 165Hz, ultra-low input lag, and VRR — it's built to keep up with the PS5 Pro's blistering speed. And those Quantum Mini LEDs deliver stunning contrast and colour, with glare-free tech keeping your view crystal clear even in a sunny room.

![a living room with a large samsung qn900f 8k tv on the wall](/images/articles/best-tvs-for-ps5/image-010.webp)

## FAQs

### Do I need HDMI 2.1 for PS5 gaming?

No, but it's recommended if you want to get the best visuals from your PS5. That's because HDMI 2.1 supports features like 4K at 120Hz, Variable Refresh Rate (VRR), and Auto Low Latency Mode (ALLM). All of which can make games look smoother and feel more responsive. But if your TV only has HDMI 2.0, you'll still get sharp 4K graphics at 60Hz.

### Is 120Hz better than 60Hz for PS5 games?

Yes, 120Hz is better if the game supports it and your TV or monitor can handle it. The higher refresh rate means the screen updates more frequently, which makes motion look less blurry.

### Should I upgrade my TV for PS5 Pro?

If your current setup doesn't support 4K at 120Hz, HDMI 2.1, or HDR, then it's definitely worth considering. The PS5 Pro is built to push better graphics and smoother gameplay than the standard model, so having a TV that can keep pace means you'll get the full experience.

### Is OLED or Mini LED better for PS5 gaming?

Both OLED and Mini LED TVs are epic for PS5 gaming, but they shine in different ways. OLED TVs offer stunning contrast and deep blacks, so they make cinematic games look incredible. While Mini-LED TVs are brighter, which means they're a better option if you play in a well-lit room. To learn more, read our Mini LED vs OLED TVs article.

### Why is input lag and response time important for PS5 gaming?

Input lag is the time it takes for your actions on the controller to show up on screen. So, the lower the lag is, the more responsive your gameplay will feel. It can make all the difference in fast-paced titles where every millisecond really matters. Response time is how quickly a TV changes pixels from one colour to another. This means that a faster response time can reduce issues like motion blur.

### How do you optimise contrast and dark details in Game Mode?

Many smart TVs have 'Game Modes' that sacrifice some picture quality to boost performance, like lowering input lag. But nothing ruins a stealth mission like murky shadows and washed-out dark scenes. And luckily, with a few tweaks, you can still get good contrast and detail with Game Mode on.

Just start by switching it on, then head to your TV's settings to adjust the brightness and contrast. Look out for options like 'local dimming' or 'black enhancer', which can help deepen blacks by turning off individual pixels or backlight zones.

### What is peak brightness and reflection handling for HDR gaming?

A TV's peak brightness is key for HDR gaming, as it affects how strongly highlights appear in your games. Look for a TV with at least 1000 nits to make the most of your PS5's HDR. That way, explosions and sun effects will really pop on-screen.

Good reflection handling is just as important, especially if you're gaming in a bright room. It reduces distracting glare on the screen, so you can feel more immersed in whatever world you're playing in. No need to dim the lights or draw the curtains!
